summary: Joel recaps a speaking event at the Hyatt Regency in Atlanta where about 110 people attended and asked great questions. He highlights meeting long-time Fog Creek community member Terry Kearns in person. He also notes that downtown Atlanta is quiet after 5pm and recommends Yelp as increasingly useful for finding restaurants, having used it to discover a nearby restaurant called Room.
key_points: About 110 people attended the Atlanta event, with some traveling from neighboring states
Meeting long-time online community members in person strengthens the relationship between a company and its supporters
Yelp was emerging as a more useful restaurant discovery tool than Chowhound
money_quotes: Downtown Atlanta is one of those places where, to put it politely, the sidewalks roll up at 5pm.
I checked Yelp, which is fast surpassing Chowhound in usefulness.
key_insight: A brief travelogue noting the value of in-person community meetups and the rise of Yelp as a local discovery platform.
